items.final_wording: Supports recording the presenting complaint, investigations or procedures, outcome, follow-up, responsible party, referral and discharge destination in a discharge handover.

source.title: Discharge to General Practice
source.jurisdiction: United Kingdom emergency and primary care; requires UAE pathway adaptation
source.population: Adult and pediatric patients being discharged directly from an emergency department.
source.setting: Emergency-department discharge communication with patients and primary care.
source.exclusions: Exact for recording the clinician-confirmed outcome, treatment plan, information communicated, required follow-up, responsible party, discharge destination and safety-net discussion. It does not make the discharge decision or generate advice.

exact_location.heading: GP Communication and example discharge-letter headings

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports recording the presenting complaint, investigations or procedures, outcome, follow-up, responsible party, referral and discharge destination in a discharge handover.

items.rationale: The exact RCEM handover section supports recording clinician-reviewed results, procedures, disposition, specialist discussion and referral or follow-up only when actually completed; it does not decide to refer.

items.final_wording: Supports signs and symptoms, allergies, medicines, past history, last intake, events or mechanism, examination, interventions, reassessment, and disposition documentation.

source.title: Basic emergency care: approach to the acutely ill and injured
source.population: Adults and children with acute illness or injury presenting to first-contact emergency care.
source.setting: First-contact emergency units and resource-limited emergency-care settings.
source.exclusions: Exact for structured trauma history, secondary survey, extremity perfusion, and open-wound assessment; partial for minor outpatient injury follow-up, imaging, closure, antibiotics, and UAE pathways.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports signs and symptoms, allergies, medicines, past history, last intake, events or mechanism, examination, interventions, reassessment, and disposition documentation.

items.rationale: The exact WHO/ICRC disposition material supports recording measured vital data, manual assessment, current severity and clinician-entered plan conveyed during referral; it does not create destination or urgency.
